phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 87.7 dBA, gradually settling to 62.7-66.7 dBA midway through session. Speech event detected at 50s mark with confidence 0.82. Moderate noise levels present; some distraction possible but manageable.
phase_1.vitals: Vitals monitoring recorded 87 bpm as session average. This deviates from ideal focus-state parameters.
phase_1.gps_motion: Location services recorded 5.2m initial error that converges to 2.3m by session midpoint. Movement remains variable with user changing posture multiple times during session.

phase_2.correlations: GPS stabilization timing precedes motion state change. Audio context remains stable throughout captured period.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Challenging environmental factors identified: 66.7 dB acoustic baseline, frequent motion, potential physiological stress.

phase_3.recommendations: Modest noise control (e.g., ambient music, quiet background) could improve focus. Moderate session length adequate; potential to extend to 363s for skill building.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (66.7dB), user-state stability (active), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (242s).
